assertions.description: window.launchQueue is exposed (the queue whose reload-time behaviour the change targets).
launchQueue.setConsumer is the API a PWA uses to receive launches; the new behaviour is observed through this consumer.
performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0].type lets script distinguish a reload from an original launch — needed to reason about LaunchParams re-queueing.

When the document type is 'reload', the consumer must not be invoked with a fresh non-empty LaunchParams from the original launch — running synchronously after registration, the page sees no replayed targetURL.
LaunchParams still exposes targetURL — the reload change is about delivery, not about removing the attribute.
navigation.currentEntry.url remains the canonical source of the current document URL after a reload that no longer re-queues LaunchParams.
